story.title: As a valid user I want to log in successfully
story.description: A standard user logs in with valid credentials and lands on the inventory page with the product list visible and no error shown. Authentication component; happy-path login. Fetched from Jira SK-10 (Mode B).

acceptance_criteria: standard_user can log in with valid credentials

ambiguities.description: The Jira issue states only the happy path (valid login succeeds). It does not specify negative behaviour (invalid password, locked-out user, empty fields). Those appear to be covered by sibling stories (SK-11 invalid login, SK-12 locked-out user), so this story's scope is treated as happy-path only. Confirm at Gate 1 that negative cases are intentionally out of scope here.

The acceptance criteria are written in the Jira issue as one compound sentence with four clauses; they have been split into four discrete AC entries verbatim by clause. Confirm this split preserves the intended meaning.

risks.description: A valid user cannot complete login at all, blocking all access to the application (authentication is the entry point to every other flow).

Login appears to succeed but the user does not actually reach a usable inventory page (e.g. the redirect lands on a blank or product-less page), so the session is created without delivering the expected landing state.
A spurious error message is shown on a successful login, eroding user trust even though access was granted.

requirements_reviewed.notes: Gate 1 approved. ACs match SK-10 verbatim; risks meaningful; happy-path-only scope confirmed (negatives belong to SK-11/SK-12).

test_scope_reviewed.notes: Gate 2 approved. 3 TCs, all approved. 100% E2E accepted as correct for a pure happy-path UI login (Saucedemo has no backend API seam); not a default-to-E2E case.

specs_reviewed.notes: Gate 3 approved. Spec matches the 3 approved TCs, locators verified live on Saucedemo, no unrelated flows, happy-path scope preserved.

code_reviewed.notes: Gate 4 approved. Stable [data-test] locators, business-behavior assertions, no skips/hard waits, one test per approved TC; suite passes live.
